vBRIEFInfo.author: overdeck/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1866: Backlog Sequencer — AI-ranked whole-backlog DAG with a reproducible markdown source of truth

plan.title: Backlog Sequencer — AI-ranked whole-backlog DAG with a reproducible markdown source of truth
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: There is no surface that shows the entire open backlog sequenced and explained. The Flywheel ranks reactively by fixed tiers + oldest-first (no holistic AI evaluation); GitHub has no real priority (tracker returns priority: undefined); and there is no reproducible, durable plan-of-record. We want an AI that ranks the whole backlog by real impact into a dependency DAG, with a repo-committed markdown source of truth anyone can reproduce.
narratives.Proposal: Add a configurable-model 'sequencer' role (tmux-hosted, spawned like the Flywheel via spawnRun) that reads the whole open backlog + the prior sequence.md + per-issue pipeline/PRD/ready flags and writes a ranked DAG to .pan/backlog/sequence.md (human table + tiered rationale + a fenced machine-readable JSON block), auto-committed to main. SQLite backlog_sequence is a disposable cache rebuildable from the md. A new Backlog dashboard page renders the sequence as a ReactFlow+dagre DAG (reusing the PlanDAG stack — no new viz dep) with in-pipeline/PRD/ready signals, condition flags (needs-refinement/stale), tiering+filters for 500+ issues, and operator pickup-gate + planning-policy controls. Three passes — creation (full), incremental (changed-only, preserves prior order), review (full re-eval) — keep re-ranking cheap; chunked map-reduce inside the agent session keeps creation/review within the context window. When auto_pickup_backlog is on and a sequence exists, the Flywheel becomes the executor of the sequence order (with emergency promotion) instead of self-ranking.
narratives.NonGoals:
- On-page dependency-edge drawing (drag node->node, source:operator) is deferred to a follow-up issue.
- 'Over-the-horizon' ghost shadow nodes are deferred.
- Bidirectional GitHub dependency sync (reading sub-issues / writing Depends-on back to GitHub) is deferred — GitHub metadata is read, never written back.
- No ranking/priority/condition is written back to the tracker; stale/needs-refinement surface for human action only and never auto-modify the tracker.
- Non-GitHub trackers are out of scope for this issue (current scope: GitHub for PAN); the design stays tracker-abstraction-friendly but is built against GitHub.
- No new frontend visualization dependency may be added — ReactFlow 11 + @dagrejs/dagre are reused.
- Intra-issue vBRIEF/beads edges are NOT used as backlog dependency edges.

autoDecisions.summary: Sequencer spawns as a tmux-hosted role run via spawnRun(runId,'sequencer',{agentId:'sequencer-runner',...}), mirroring spawnFlywheelAgent.
autoDecisions.rationale: Every role run in this codebase is a tmux Claude session; there is no direct-LLM-API precedent for role runs (verified in agents.ts/flywheel.ts). Reusing the Flywheel singleton-agent pattern gives lifecycle, observability, and model-routing for free.

autoDecisions.summary: Chunked map-reduce (NFR-6) is realized inside the agent session via a compact manifest + a batched body accessor, not server-side multi-call fan-out.
autoDecisions.rationale: The full backlog (~370k tokens) exceeds one context window; with no direct-API path, the cleanest fit is one agent that reads bodies in batches against a running shortlist. Bodies are never all inlined, satisfying NFR-6, while keeping a single ranker/owner of the order.

autoDecisions.summary: in-pipeline is computed server-side from review_status (non-pending) OR live workspace/branch/agent, not by importing the frontend derivePipelineState helper.
autoDecisions.rationale: derivePipelineState lives in the frontend; importing it into the Effect server breaks the build. Recomputing from the same inputs server-side reuses existing signals without inventing state.

autoDecisions.summary: On-demand pass buttons are the acceptance-critical MVP; the debounced delta auto-trigger + periodic review cadence is the lowest-priority bead and may land last.
autoDecisions.rationale: The PRD marks the auto-trigger 'a follow-up wire-up, not a blocker'. On-demand creation/incremental/review fully satisfies the FR-10 acceptance criteria.

autoDecisions.summary: Operator gate 'blocked' reuses the existing parked-label mechanism (BLOCKER_LABELS) rather than a new hold system.
autoDecisions.rationale: The PRD calls for honoring the existing Flywheel skip immediately; reusing BLOCKER_LABELS avoids a parallel system and is consistent with auto-merge-eligibility.

items.title: Define sequence.md JSON schema + TypeScript types

narrative.Action: Create src/lib/backlog/types.ts defining the canonical types + a runtime validator for the machine-readable JSON block of .pan/backlog/sequence.md. Top-level: { version, project, generatedAt, model, pass in {creation,incremental,review}, lastReviewPass, openCount, nodes[], edges[] }. Node: { issue, rank, size in {XS,S,M,L,XL}, importance in {critical,high,medium,low}, score 0-100, condition in {ok,needs-refinement,stale}, dependsOn[], why (<=140 chars), rationale? (top-tier only), gate in {auto,ready,blocked}, planning in {skip,auto,interactive} }. Edge: { from, to, type in {unblocks,informs}, source in {github-ref,operator,ai-inferred}, confidence }. Export a parseSequenceJson(value) validator returning a typed SequenceDoc or a typed error. This is the cross-cutting contract the parser, writer, cache, route, and agent all encode assumptions about — keep it stable.

items.title: Exports a SequenceDoc type and a parseSequenceJson validator that returns a typed doc for a well-formed object and rejects an object whose node omits issue, rank, size, importance, or condition

The validator accepts exactly the documented enum sets (size XS-XL, importance critical-low, condition ok/needs-refinement/stale, gate auto/ready/blocked, planning skip/auto/interactive, edge source github-ref/operator/ai-inferred) and rejects out-of-enum values
The validator rejects a node whose why field exceeds 140 characters
Implement parseSequenceMd (extract + validate fenced JSON block)

narrative.Action: Implement parseSequenceMd(markdown): SequenceDoc | ParseError in src/lib/backlog/sequence-io.ts. Locate the fenced ```json block below the '<!-- machine-readable; do not hand-edit below this line -->' marker, parse it, run it through parseSequenceJson from types.ts, and recover from a malformed/missing block by returning a typed parse error rather than throwing. The human table/prose above the marker is ignored — only the fenced JSON block is authoritative.

items.title: Given a sequence.md with a valid fenced JSON block, parseSequenceMd returns a SequenceDoc whose nodes length equals the block's node count
Given a sequence.md whose JSON block is truncated or absent, parseSequenceMd returns a typed parse-error result and does not throw
Given a sequence.md whose human table disagrees with the fenced JSON block, parseSequenceMd returns the values from the JSON block, ignoring the table/prose above the marker
Implement writeSequenceMd (render table + tiered rationale + JSON, auto-commit)

narrative.Action: Implement writeSequenceMd(projectRoot, doc) in src/lib/backlog/sequence-io.ts. Render: a header line (last-sequenced timestamp + model + open count); a compact ranked table (rank/issue/size/importance/condition/deps/short why); a '## Rationale detail' section with full paragraphs for the active top tier only (~top 80); and the fenced machine-readable JSON block below the do-not-hand-edit marker. Write to .pan/backlog/sequence.md and call queueAutoCommit({projectRoot, paths:['.pan/backlog/sequence.md'], subject:'chore(state): update backlog sequence (<project>)'}). Store each rationale exactly once (full paragraph only for top tier; one-line why for the tail) to honor the token budget.

items.title: writeSequenceMd produces a file that parseSequenceMd round-trips back to a SequenceDoc equal to the input doc (ignoring the generatedAt timestamp)
The writer emits a full-paragraph rationale only for top-tier nodes and a one-line why for every node, and a footprint test asserts a 522-node document stays <= ~65k tokens
writeSequenceMd commits the file via queueAutoCommit with subject 'chore(state): update backlog sequence (<project>)'
collectOpenBacklog — manifest + batched body accessor + flag joins

narrative.Action: Implement collectOpenBacklog(projectKey) in src/lib/backlog/backlog-input.ts. List all open issues via IssueDataService.getIssues()/tracker.listIssues({includeClosed:false}) excluding closed/cancelled. For each issue join: inPipeline (review_status non-pending OR live workspace/branch/agent), hasPrd (hasIssueDraft from src/lib/pan-dir/drafts.ts), ready (findSpecByIssue present AND computeBeadCounts non-null). Include the GitHub priority field as ONE signal and issue age. Load the prior .pan/backlog/sequence.md when present. Return a compact manifest (id, title, labels, priority-signal, age, inPipeline, hasPrd, ready) PLUS a batched body accessor so a consumer reads N bodies in K batches rather than one inlined concatenation (NFR-6).

items.title: collectOpenBacklog returns one manifest entry per open issue and excludes issues whose tracker state is closed or cancelled
Each manifest entry contains inPipeline, hasPrd, and ready flags derived from review_status/workspace, hasIssueDraft, and findSpecByIssue+computeBeadCounts respectively
collectOpenBacklog returns a batched body accessor (not a single inlined concatenation of all bodies), so a caller reads N bodies in K batches
When a prior .pan/backlog/sequence.md exists, collectOpenBacklog loads and returns it alongside the manifest
Register the 'sequencer' role in config (Role union + defaults)

narrative.Action: Add 'sequencer' to the Role union in src/lib/agents.ts (~line 67); add sequencer: 'workhorse:expensive' to DEFAULT_MODEL_REFS and sequencer: { model: 'workhorse:expensive' } to DEFAULT_ROLES in src/lib/config-yaml.ts (~lines 425 and 443). The model resolves at spawn via determineModel({role:'sequencer'}) -> resolveModel -> derefWorkhorse.

items.title: resolveModel('sequencer', undefined, {}) returns the expensive-workhorse default (claude-opus-4-8) when no override is configured
Setting roles.sequencer.model in config and calling resolveModel('sequencer', ...) returns the configured model, overriding the default
The project typecheck passes with 'sequencer' added to the Role union, and every exhaustive switch over Role still compiles
Author roles/sequencer.md + buildSequencerPrompt(pass, input)

narrative.Action: Author roles/sequencer.md (the role prompt) plus a buildSequencerPrompt(pass, input) assembler in src/lib/backlog/sequencer-agent.ts that injects the pass + backlog input. The prompt must instruct the agent to: read the prior sequence.md + the backlog manifest; for creation/review, read issue bodies in BATCHES against a running shortlist then merge (never inline the whole backlog); for incremental, read only changed issues and preserve prior order/rationale unless a delta justifies change (each change carries a rationale); assign every issue a condition (ok/needs-refinement/stale); never re-rank pinned in-pipeline issues; preserve operator-owned gate/planning fields and operator-sourced edges verbatim, re-derive github-ref edges, recompute ai-inferred edges as advisory; rank by impact with GitHub priority + age as inputs (not determinants); stamp pass + generatedAt; write via writeSequenceMd.

items.title: buildSequencerPrompt('incremental', input) returns a prompt string containing the preserve-prior-order and read-only-changed-issues instructions
buildSequencerPrompt('creation', input) returns a prompt that instructs batched body reads against a running shortlist and explicitly forbids inlining the entire backlog in one prompt
The built prompt for any pass contains instructions to assign each issue a condition and to keep operator-owned gate/planning fields and operator-sourced edges verbatim
The built prompt contains a rank-by-impact instruction that treats GitHub priority and issue age as inputs only, not as the sole determinant
spawnSequencerAgent(pass) — tmux role run mirroring the Flywheel

narrative.Action: Implement spawnSequencerAgent(pass, opts) in src/lib/backlog/sequencer-agent.ts, mirroring spawnFlywheelAgent (src/lib/cloister/flywheel.ts:117). Call spawnRun(runId, 'sequencer', { agentId: 'sequencer-runner', workspace, prompt: buildSequencerPrompt(pass, collectOpenBacklog(project)), model: opts.model, allowHost: true, registerConversation: true }). Select creation automatically when no .pan/backlog/sequence.md exists, else default incremental; accept review explicitly. Model resolves through determineModel({role:'sequencer'}).

items.title: spawnSequencerAgent launches a tmux-hosted role run with role 'sequencer' and the singleton agent id 'sequencer-runner', resolving its model from roles.sequencer.model
When no .pan/backlog/sequence.md exists the spawn defaults the pass to creation; when one exists it defaults to incremental; a review pass can be requested explicitly
The prompt passed to spawnRun contains the selected pass and the backlog input produced by collectOpenBacklog
Surface the Sequencer role in Settings → Roles

metadata.acJustification: A settings row has exactly two observable behaviors: it renders with the default, and it persists/round-trips a change.

narrative.Action: Add a 'Sequencer' RoleDefinition to the ROLES array in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Settings/RolesPanel.tsx (~line 88): { id:'sequencer', name:'Sequencer', icon:<lucide icon>, description:'Ranks the whole open backlog into a reproducible DAG.', defaultModel:'workhorse:expensive' }. This makes roles.sequencer.model selectable and round-trips through GET/PUT /api/settings.

items.title: Settings → Roles renders a 'Sequencer' row whose model selector defaults to the expensive workhorse
Changing the Sequencer model and saving persists roles.sequencer.model to config and the new value is read back on the next settings fetch
backlog_sequence SQLite cache table + rebuild-from-md

narrative.Action: Add the backlog_sequence cache table in src/lib/database/schema.ts: bump SCHEMA_VERSION 56->57; add CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS backlog_sequence (...) in initSchema(); add an additive `if (currentVersion < 57)` block in runMigrations(). Implement rebuildBacklogSequenceFromMd(projectRoot) that parses .pan/backlog/sequence.md (parseSequenceMd) and repopulates the table. The table is a disposable cache fully derivable from the md.

items.title: Deleting all backlog_sequence rows and running rebuildBacklogSequenceFromMd repopulates the table to a state equivalent to the prior contents, purely from sequence.md
Opening a database at schema version 56 applies the version-57 migration and creates the backlog_sequence table without disturbing existing tables
GET /api/backlog/sequence — joined view + route registration

narrative.Action: Create src/dashboard/server/routes/backlog.ts following the Effect.js HttpRouter pattern in routes/costs.ts. Add GET /api/backlog/sequence: read the parsed sequence (from the backlog_sequence cache, falling back to parseSequenceMd), join per-issue inPipeline/hasPrd/ready live state server-side (do NOT import the frontend derivePipelineState), and return the joined view as JSON. Export backlogRouteLayer = Layer.mergeAll(...) and register it in makeRoutesLayer in src/dashboard/server/server.ts (~line 297).

items.title: GET /api/backlog/sequence returns the parsed sequence nodes joined with per-issue inPipeline/hasPrd/ready flags as JSON
With the SQLite cache emptied, the endpoint reproduces the identical sequence view purely by re-parsing .pan/backlog/sequence.md
backlogRouteLayer is registered in makeRoutesLayer and the server typechecks
POST /api/backlog/sequence/regenerate — trigger a pass

metadata.acJustification: A trigger endpoint has two observable behaviors: it spawns on an explicit pass and it defaults the pass correctly.

narrative.Action: Add POST /api/backlog/sequence/regenerate to routes/backlog.ts accepting an optional pass in {creation,incremental,review}; invoke spawnSequencerAgent(pass) and return the spawned run id/status as JSON.

items.title: POST /api/backlog/sequence/regenerate with pass=incremental spawns a sequencer run and returns its run identifier
Omitting pass defaults to incremental when a sequence exists and creation when none does
POST operator pickup-gate + planning-policy — persist to sequence.md

narrative.Action: Add operator-field endpoints to routes/backlog.ts that set the operator-owned pickup gate (auto/ready/blocked) and planning policy (skip/auto/interactive) for a single issue, persisting them into .pan/backlog/sequence.md via writeSequenceMd WITHOUT a full re-sequence (these fields are operator-owned and preserved verbatim by the sequencer). When gate=blocked, also apply the existing parked label (BLOCKER_LABELS from src/lib/cloister/auto-merge-eligibility.ts) so the current Flywheel skip honors it immediately.

items.title: Setting an issue's gate to ready or blocked persists the value into sequence.md and a subsequent GET /api/backlog/sequence returns the new gate
Setting gate blocked applies the existing parked label so the current Flywheel skip honors it
Setting an issue's planning policy persists planning into sequence.md and the value survives a re-read
Wire the new Backlog top-level tab + page shell

narrative.Action: Wire a new top-level Backlog tab: add 'backlog' to the Tab union in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Header.tsx (~line 4); add backlog:'/backlog' to TAB_PATHS and a render branch in src/dashboard/frontend/src/App.tsx (~lines 88 and ~1462+); add a Sidebar nav item + lucide icon in src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/Sidebar.tsx (~line 84). Create src/dashboard/frontend/src/pages/BacklogSequencerPage.tsx (template: pages/FlywheelPage.tsx) that fetches GET /api/backlog/sequence and renders a placeholder list of nodes.

items.title: Navigating to /backlog (sidebar item or path) renders the BacklogSequencerPage
The page fetches GET /api/backlog/sequence on mount and renders one entry per returned node
The frontend typecheck passes with 'backlog' added to the Tab union and TAB_PATHS
BacklogDAG + IssueNode (ReactFlow + dagre, reused stack)

narrative.Action: Implement src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/backlog/BacklogDAG.tsx + a custom IssueNode, reusing ReactFlow 11 + @dagrejs/dagre (TB layout) exactly like src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/PlanDAG.tsx — no new viz dependency. Encode per node: rank badge (mono, top-left), AI size (node width/height), importance left-border heat (red->amber->neutral), in-pipeline glowing ring + live verb badge (reuse the .plan-glow precedent), 📄 PRD chip, ✓ READY chip, directed dependency edges (unblocks solid / informs dashed). Clicking a node opens a side panel showing the issue's rationale from the sequence. Follow the style guide (DM Sans, font-medium, semantic tokens, signal-color discipline).

items.title: The DAG renders each issue as a node sized by AI size with a rank badge and an importance-colored left border, laid out top-to-bottom via dagre
In-pipeline nodes render a glowing ring + live badge; the PRD chip and READY chip render only when those flags are present in the joined data
Dependency edges render directed, with unblocks solid and informs dashed
Clicking a node opens a side panel that displays that issue's rationale from the sequence
Tiered rendering + filters for a 500+ issue backlog

narrative.Action: Add tiered rendering + filters to the Backlog page so a 500+ backlog stays usable. Default to a top-tier view: top N + dependency neighbors + always the pinned/in-pipeline set. Render as a DAG up to a ~150-node budget; beyond it, fall back to a virtualized ranked list. Collapse the long tail behind a visible count. Add filters for importance, condition, in-pipeline, and ready. Tiers (Now/Next/Later/Someday) are DERIVED bands over rank/score, not AI-assigned.

items.title: With 500+ nodes the page renders only the top-tier-plus-neighbors set by default and collapses the remainder behind a visible count
Switching to a tier whose node count exceeds ~150 renders a virtualized ranked list instead of a DAG
Each filter (importance, condition, in-pipeline, ready) renders only the issues matching the selected facet
Condition indicators (REFINE / STALE) + candidates-to-close summary

narrative.Action: Render condition treatments on nodes + the rationale panel: needs-refinement -> amber '⚠ REFINE' indicator with a 'Draft PRD' action; stale -> dimmed/strikethrough '⊘ STALE' candidate-to-close treatment with a 'Close issue' action. Add a 'candidates to close' summary listing every stale issue. Neither indicator auto-modifies the tracker — the actions are explicit and operator-initiated. Amber = 'a human must act' per the style guide.

items.title: A needs-refinement issue renders the amber ⚠ REFINE indicator and offers a Draft-PRD action; a stale issue renders dimmed with ⊘ STALE and offers a Close-issue action
Neither indicator updates the tracker on its own; the Draft-PRD and Close-issue actions fire only when the operator clicks them
A 'candidates to close' summary shows every issue whose condition is stale
Operator pickup-gate + planning-policy controls on the node panel

narrative.Action: Add a segmented Auto / ✓ Ready / ⛔ Block pickup-gate control and a skip / auto / interactive planning-policy control to the node rationale panel. Nodes display '📌 PROMOTED' when gate=ready and '⛔ HELD' when gate=blocked. The controls POST to the operator-fields route (backlog-operator-fields-route).

items.title: The rationale panel shows a segmented gate control; choosing ✓ Ready or ⛔ Block calls the operator-fields endpoint and the node updates to 📌 PROMOTED / ⛔ HELD
The planning-policy control persists the chosen skip/auto/interactive value via the same route
A node's gate/planning selection survives a page reload, read back from the persisted sequence
Flywheel executes the sequence order under auto_pickup_backlog

narrative.Action: In src/lib/cloister/flywheel.ts (+ src/lib/flywheel-merge-order.ts), when flywheel.auto_pickup_backlog is enabled AND a .pan/backlog/sequence.md exists, make the Flywheel the executor: pick the highest-ranked ELIGIBLE issue from the parsed sequence (eligible = gate != blocked, not parked, not in-pipeline, passes the EXISTING author/assignee security filter, ready-or-has-PRD), and bypass its own P0-P3/oldest-first ranking (kept only as the no-sequence fallback). Add emergency promotion: when it finds a new pipeline-critical issue, file it, set gate:ready, and trigger an incremental pass (spawnSequencerAgent('incremental')) WITHOUT re-ranking the rest. CRITICAL: the existing security filter must still gate every pick (hazard H3).

items.title: With auto_pickup_backlog on and a sequence present, the Flywheel spawns work for the highest-ranked eligible issue from the sequence rather than its oldest-first pick
The executor refuses to pick a blocked, parked, in-pipeline, or security-filtered (unsolicited third-party) issue from the sequence
With no .pan/backlog/sequence.md present, the Flywheel falls back to its existing P0-P3/oldest-first ranking
Emergency promotion marks a new pipeline-critical issue gate:ready and spawns an incremental pass without re-ranking other issues
(Follow-up) debounced delta auto-trigger + periodic review cadence

metadata.acJustification: A wire-up bead with two observable behaviors: a debounced incremental on delta and a periodic review cadence.

narrative.Action: Wire the auto-trigger (on-demand buttons remain the acceptance-critical MVP, so this lands last). Add a debounced incremental-pass trigger on backlog deltas (issue opened/closed/edited) via IssueDataService, plus a configurable periodic review-pass cadence. Both call spawnSequencerAgent.

items.title: A backlog delta (issue opened/closed/edited) spawns a single debounced incremental sequencer pass rather than one pass per change
A configurable cadence spawns a periodic review pass
